core_mechanism: a self-consistent perturbative-renormalization treatment of the spectrum of a linear diffusion (Laplacian) operator when a small density of quenched long-range random couplings is added to a regular lattice, in which the mean-field/annealed result is the zeroth-order term and higher-order resummation captures the breakdown of mean-field scaling and a transient-vs-recurrent phase transition
brief_reason: The abstract builds its method by treating the added random links perturbatively so that the mean-field (annealed) prediction is the lowest-order term of the expansion, then resumming them self-consistently via a renormalization approach on the network Laplacian to recover the finite-size crossover and phase diagram.
